abstract: Sets the specified JavaScript property of the context’s global object, allowing subscript setter syntax.

declaration.code: func setObject(_ object: Any!, forKeyedSubscript key: (any NSCopying & NSObjectProtocol)!)

overview:
## Discussion

This method first constructs a [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue] object from the `key` parameter, then uses that value in JavaScript to set the property in the context’s global object.

Use this method (or Objective-C subscript syntax) to bridge native objects or functions for use in JavaScript. For example, the following code creates a JavaScript function whose implementation is an Objective-C block:

items.description: Returns the value of the specified JavaScript property in the context’s global object, allowing subscript getter syntax.
items.name: objectForKeyedSubscript(_:)

sections.title: Accessing JavaScript global state with subscripts

title: setObject(_:forKeyedSubscript:)
